# MDE-QRCode

> A Google Material Design 3 (M3) Expressive style QR Code generator for Node.js.

## Overview

MDE-QRCode generates QR codes with rounded, fluid corners inspired by Google's Material Design 3 Expressive visual language. It produces high-quality SVG, PNG, and JPEG outputs with customizable styling, including two independent corner radius controls and optional center logo embedding.

- Repository: https://github.com/origami-suki/MDE-QRCode
- Language: TypeScript (Node.js)
- License: MIT
- Built with: Antigravity CLI + Gemini 3.5 Flash

## Installation

```bash
git clone https://github.com/origami-suki/MDE-QRCode.git
cd MDE-QRCode
npm install
```

## CLI Usage

The CLI can be invoked in two ways:

```bash
npm start -- [options]
# or
node dist/cli.js [options]
```

### All CLI Options

```
--text <string>       Text or URL to encode (default: "https://github.com/origami-suki/MDE-QRCode")
--out <path>          Output file path; extension determines format: .svg, .png, .jpg (default: "qrcode.svg")
--logo <url/path>     URL or local file path to a center logo image (default: none)
--radius <float>      Outer convex corner roundness, 0.0 (square) to 1.0 (circle) (default: 1.0)
--radius2 <float>     Inner connected corner roundness, 0.0 (sharp) to 1.0 (full round) (default: 0.15)
--size <number>       Output width/height in pixels (default: 512)
--margin <number>     Quiet zone margin in cell modules (default: 4)
--primary <hex>       Primary module color as hex string (default: "#1D1B20")
--bg <hex>            Background color as hex string (default: "#FFFFFF")
--level <L|M|Q|H>     Error correction level (default: "H")
-h, --help            Show help message
```

### CLI Examples

```bash
# Basic SVG QR code
npm start -- --text "https://example.com" --out example.svg

# High-res PNG with custom color
npm start -- --text "Hello World" --out hello.png --size 1024 --primary "#6750A4"

# With logo and relaxed rounding
npm start -- --text "https://github.com" --out github.svg --logo "logo.png" --radius 0.8 --radius2 0.2

# Fully square modules, no connected rounding
npm start -- --text "test" --out square.svg --radius 0 --radius2 0

# JPEG output with lower error correction
npm start -- --text "data" --out data.jpg --level M
```

## Library API

### `generateMDEQRCodeSVG(options: MDEQRCodeOptions): Promise<string>`

Returns the QR code as an SVG string.

### `generateMDEQRCode(options: MDEQRCodeOptions, format?: MDEQRCodeFormat): Promise<string | Buffer>`

Returns the QR code in the specified format. Format can be `'svg'`, `'png'`, `'jpg'`, or `'jpeg'`. SVG returns a string; raster formats return a Buffer.

### MDEQRCodeOptions Interface

```typescript
interface MDEQRCodeOptions {
  text: string;                                    // Required. Text or URL to encode.
  size?: number;                                   // Output size in pixels. Default: 512.
  margin?: number;                                 // Quiet zone margin in cell modules. Default: 4.
  primaryColor?: string;                           // Module fill color. Default: "#1D1B20".
  backgroundColor?: string;                        // Background color. Default: "#FFFFFF".
  errorCorrectionLevel?: 'L' | 'M' | 'Q' | 'H';   // Error correction level. Default: "H".
  logoUrl?: string;                                // URL or path to center logo image.
  fluidRadius?: number;                            // Outer corner roundness, 0.0–1.0. Default: 1.0.
  fluidRadius2?: number;                           // Connected corner roundness, 0.0–1.0. Default: 0.15.
}
```

### Library Examples

```typescript
import { generateMDEQRCodeSVG, generateMDEQRCode } from 'mde-qrcode';

// Example 1: Generate SVG string with defaults
const svg = await generateMDEQRCodeSVG({
  text: 'https://example.com',
});

// Example 2: Generate SVG with full customization
const customSvg = await generateMDEQRCodeSVG({
  text: 'https://google.com',
  size: 800,
  margin: 2,
  primaryColor: '#4285F4',
  backgroundColor: '#F8F9FA',
  fluidRadius: 0.8,
  fluidRadius2: 0.2,
  errorCorrectionLevel: 'Q',
});

// Example 3: Generate PNG buffer
const pngBuffer = await generateMDEQRCode({
  text: 'Hello World',
  size: 1024,
  primaryColor: '#6750A4',
  backgroundColor: '#FEF7FF',
}, 'png');

// Example 4: Generate with center logo
const logoSvg = await generateMDEQRCodeSVG({
  text: 'https://github.com',
  logoUrl: 'https://github.githubassets.com/favicons/favicon.png',
  errorCorrectionLevel: 'H', // Use H for logo tolerance
});
```

## Technical Details

### Radius Parameters Explained

- **`fluidRadius` / `--radius`**: Controls the convex (outer) corners of individual dark modules. At `1.0`, each isolated module becomes a perfect circle. At `0.0`, modules are square. When a module has an adjacent neighbor, the shared edge becomes straight (radius is set to 0 on that corner) so modules merge seamlessly.

- **`fluidRadius2` / `--radius2`**: Controls the concave (inner) corners where dark modules meet at a diagonal. When two dark modules share an L-shaped or T-shaped connection, the inner corner of the white cell at the diagonal can be filled with a smooth arc. At `0.0`, these inner corners are sharp. At higher values, they become rounded, creating a more fluid, organic appearance.

### Finder Patterns

The three QR code finder patterns (top-left, top-right, bottom-left) use Material Design 3 Expressive styling:
- Outer frame: Squircle (rounded rectangle with `2.2 × cellSize` corner radius)
- Inner ring: Rounded rectangle with `1.2 × cellSize` corner radius, cut out via `evenodd` fill rule
- Center eye: Perfect circle with `1.5 × cellSize` radius

### Output Formats

| Format | Extension | Returns | Notes |
|--------|-----------|---------|-------|
| SVG | `.svg` | `string` | Vector, scalable, default |
| PNG | `.png` | `Buffer` | Rasterized via `@resvg/resvg-js` |
| JPEG | `.jpg` / `.jpeg` | `Buffer` | Converted from PNG via `jimp` |

### Dependencies

- `qrcode` — QR matrix generation
- `@resvg/resvg-js` — SVG to PNG rasterization
- `jimp` — PNG to JPEG conversion

## Project Structure

```
MDE-QRCode/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts      # Core QR code generation logic (SVG builder)
│   ├── cli.ts        # CLI argument parser and entry point
│   └── colors.ts     # Material Design 3 color constants
├── dist/             # Compiled JavaScript output (generated by tsc)
├── output/           # Default output directory for generated QR codes
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── llms.txt          # This file — AI-readable documentation
```
